title: Revenue Radar: BlackBerry
text: BlackBerry closed out the transformational fiscal year with strong financial performance from all three divisions: QNX, Secure Communications and Licensing, and completed the sale of the Cylance business to Arctic Wolf, significantly strengthening its balance sheet. The company started the year with a goal to deliver profitability and positive cash flow, and delivered across the board. Q4 revenue reached $141.7 million with 74% adjusted gross margin, with all three divisions exceeding guidance. QNX revenue of $65.8 million with 83% gross margin, Secure Communications revenue of $67.3 million, and Licensing revenue of $8.6 million. BlackBerry's cash position strengthened to $410 million. In Q2 2025, BlackBerry reached breakeven adjusted EBITDA and non-GAAP EPS through double-digit revenue growth for both IoT and Cybersecurity, with operating expenses 24% lower than the baseline for the prior year. BlackBerry significantly reduced its cash burn, with operating cash usage improving by $43 million year-over-year to $13 million. The company delivered stronger than expected profitability and a return to positive cash flow ahead of schedule. Q1 2025 IoT revenue rose 18% to $53M, with cybersecurity ARR at $285M, driven by high-demand sectors like healthcare and finance.
